summary: Fair‑quality audience tape of a solid show with a smoking Help > Slipknot! > Franklin's sequence and strong New Speedway Boogie, but the first set is hampered by over‑aggressive keyboards.
review: The recording is an audience capture rated about C+, which gives it a fair level of clarity but includes typical crowd ambience. Musically the show shines with a soulful Jerry Garcia vocal moment in SOTM and a standout Help > Slipknot! > Franklin's run, plus a powerful New Speedway Boogie; however reviewers note that Brent Hornsby’s aggressive keyboard work overpowers the first set before easing off in the second.

ai_rating.rationale: Two reviewers praised the performance (4‑star each) while one gave a low rating due to the C+ audience recording; combining strong show elements with only fair audio yields an overall mid‑high score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: C+ audience tape – captures crowd ambience but lacks the crispness of a soundboard; some muddiness and limited detail, no major dropouts mentioned.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set feels crowded by overly aggressive keyboard playing that masks Garcia and Weir; second set eases off, giving the band more space and a better overall balance.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His raspy, soulful vocal line toward the end of SOTM and subsequent guitar solo were highlighted as emotional highlights.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Hornsby (keys) was criticized for being too aggressive in set 1, overrunning riffs and muting other instruments, though his enthusiasm is noted.
